OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with knee pain following recent increased physical activity.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents with knee pain that started yesterday and has been steadily worsening. She cites recent participation in volleyball involving repetitive pivoting and twisting movements as a possible cause. She was advised at her last physical exam that weight loss could be beneficial for her health.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Knee pain, specifically over the medial aspect of the tibia just inferior to the patella

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Polycystic ovarian syndrome (PCOS), currently managed with oral contraceptive pills.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Has recently joined a volleyball team.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any fever, recent injuries, swelling or discoloration of the knee, or previous similar complaints.

Lower_Extremity_Examination.Inspection: No visible abnormalities, erythema, or swelling.
Lower_Extremity_Examination.Palpation: Tenderness over the medial aspect of the tibia just inferior to the patella.
Lower_Extremity_Examination.Range_of_Motion: Full range of motion without significant pain or limitation.

X-Ray_Knee.Findings: No fracture, dislocation, or appreciable joint effusion.

MRI_Knee.Optional: Can be considered if clinical suspicion persists despite negative initial workup, not mandatory for diagnosis in this simulated case.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with a breast mass.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient noticed a mass in her left breast 4 weeks ago, which has rapidly increased in size. She denies any recent trauma or infections. No history of similar complaints in the past.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Presence of a left breast mass
Secondary_Symptoms: Rapid increase in size of the mass
No pain associated with the mass

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical or surgical history. No family history of breast cancer.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Non-smoker, moderate alcohol use. Elementary school teacher.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, weight loss, night sweats, or changes in appetite.

Breast_Examination.Inspection: No skin changes, nipple retraction, or discharge.
Breast_Examination.Palpation: A 6-cm, nontender, multinodular mass palpated in the upper outer quadrant of the left breast. No palpable axillary or cervical lymphadenopathy.

Mammography.Findings: Smooth polylobulated mass in the left breast.

Biopsy.Microscopic_Description: Image shows leaf-like structures with stromal cellular overgrowth.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with chronic left shoulder pain without a history of trauma.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports a 2-month history of insidious onset left shoulder pain, worsening with extreme ranges of motion and activity. Pain disrupts sleep, and there's noted difficulty with activities of daily living like brushing hair and dressing. No history of shoulder trauma, neck pain, arm/hand weakness, numbness, or paresthesias. The patient has type 2 diabetes mellitus, treated with metformin and glipizide.

Secondary_Symptoms: Difficulty sleeping on the affected side
Difficulty with brushing hair and dressing

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Type 2 diabetes mellitus
Patient_Actor.Social_History: No relevant social history provided
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies neck pain, arm/hand weakness, numbness, or paresthesias.

Shoulder_Examination.Palpation: Tenderness around the shoulder joint, no warmth or crepitus.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with mental status changes.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has been brought to the emergency room by her family due to altered mental status. The family mentions that she has been increasingly confused over the past few days. No recent head trauma or known drug use. The patient has a smoking history of 30 pack-years.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Patient has a history of hypertension, managed with medication. No other significant medical history is reported.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Patient is a smoker with a 30 pack-year history. Drinks alcohol socially. No illicit drug use is reported.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies headache, seizure, vision changes, chest pain, dyspnea, nausea, vomiting, recent illness, or fever.

Neurological_Examination.Mental_Status: Alert but noticeably confused. Oriented to person but not to time or place.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with a pruritic rash on trunk and extremities, recent history of low-grade fever, rhinorrhea, and headache.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has been experiencing a mildly pruritic rash on her trunk and extremities for 2 days. One week ago, she developed symptoms of low-grade fever, rhinorrhea, and headache. A facial rash appeared 4 days later, sparing the perioral area.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Mildly pruritic rash on trunk and extremities
Secondary_Symptoms: Facial rash not involving the perioral skin

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Attends local school. Lives with parents and one younger sibling. No recent travel history.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any respiratory distress, joint pains, or changes in bowel or urinary habits.

Skin_Examination.Observation: Lacy, reticular rash noted on the trunk and extremities. Facial rash present but sparing the perioral area.
Skin_Examination.Palpation: Rash is slightly raised but not accompanied by significant warmth or tenderness.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with fatigue, difficulty to concentrate, and abnormal blood findings.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has a 3-week history of fatigue, difficulty to concentrate, and dyspnea on exertion. He also experiences dizziness and digital pain that improves with cold. He has a long history of smoking.

Secondary_Symptoms: Digital pain that improves with cold

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Hypertension, currently managed with enalapril.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Smokes half a pack of cigarettes a day since he was 20.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: The patient denies any recent infections, bleeding disorders, or significant weight loss.

General_Examination.Observation: No acute distress, appears mildly fatigued

Cardiovascular_System.Observation: No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. Peripheral pulses are intact.

Respiratory_System.Auscultation: Clear bilateral breath sounds without wheezes, crackles, or rhonchi.

Complete_Blood_Count.Other_Findings: Blood smear shows increased abnormal platelets.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with episodic cough and shortness of breath.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports a 1-month history of episodic cough and shortness of breath. The cough is nonproductive and worsens when climbing stairs and at night. She experienced fever, sore throat, and nasal congestion 8 weeks ago.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Episodic cough and shortness of breath
Secondary_Symptoms: Cough worsens with exertion and at night
History of recent upper respiratory tract infection symptoms such as fever, sore throat, and nasal congestion

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: 10-year history of hypertension, currently managed with enalapril. History of smoking half a pack of cigarettes daily for 16 years.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Smoker, half a pack of cigarettes daily for 16 years.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies chest pain or palpitations.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with a pruritic rash on the bilateral upper extremities.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient complains of a pruritic rash on both upper extremities that has been present for 5 months. She notes that the rash occasionally worsens but has not completely resolved at any point. She denies any recent illnesses and mentions that she has not started any new medications or come into contact with any new substances that she is aware of.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Pruritic rash on the bilateral upper extremities

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No history of serious illness. No known drug allergies. Takes no medications.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: College student studying graphic design. Non-smoker, drinks alcohol socially. No recreational drug use.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies recent fever, joint pains, weight loss, or changes in bowel habits.

Skin_Examination.Inspection: Bilateral erythematous, scaly, and crusted lesions localized to the upper extremities.
Skin_Examination.Palpation: No warmth or induration. The lesions are slightly raised and rough to touch.

Skin_Biopsy.Findings: Intraepidermal accumulation of edematous fluid and widening of intercellular spaces between keratinocytes.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate the patient presenting with persistent bleeding post wisdom tooth extraction and history of easy bruising for possible bleeding disorders.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient was brought in by his mother for persistent bleeding following elective removal of wisdom teeth. The bleeding from the surrounding gums was minimally responsive to multiple gauze packs. The patient also has a history of easy bruising. Family history includes the patient's uncle who had similar issues with bleeding and a history of easy bruising and joint swelling.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Persistent bleeding from gums following tooth extraction
Secondary_Symptoms: Family history of similar bleeding problems and joint swelling

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Previously healthy, takes no medications.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: The patient is a high school student with no history of smoking, alcohol, or drug use.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, joint pains (except noted in family history), or significant past illnesses.

Oral_Examination.Gums: Persistent oozing noted from the site of wisdom tooth extraction.
Oral_Examination.Mucosa: No other visible lesions or bruises.

Skin_Examination.Bruises: Several old bruises of different ages noted on the extremities.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess the patient and determine the cause of jaundice, pruritus, weight loss, pale stool, and dark urine.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has experienced gradual yellow discoloration of the skin and generalized pruritus over the past 2 weeks. He also reports a decreased appetite and a weight loss of 6.3 kg (14 lb). The patient’s stool has been pale, and his urine is very dark. Three years ago, he had an episode of acute upper abdominal pain, which was managed with IV fluids, NSAIDs, and dietary modification. The patient has abstained from alcohol for these three years, after having consumed 1–2 beers daily for 40 years. He has a 50-year history of smoking a pack of cigarettes daily.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Episode of acute upper abdominal pain treated three years ago. History of long-term alcohol consumption and smoking.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Stopped alcohol consumption three years ago; 50-year history of smoking a pack of cigarettes daily.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: No additional symptoms were reported.

Abdominal_Examination.Inspection: Jaundice noted in the conjunctivae and skin.
Abdominal_Examination.Palpation: No tenderness, but a soft, cystic mass palpated in the right upper quadrant.

Abdominal_Ultrasonography.Findings: Anechoic cystic mass in the subhepatic region. Dilation of the intrahepatic and extrahepatic bile ducts.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with unusual behavior and beliefs regarding telepathic communication with animals.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents with a chief complaint of 'failing health.' He reports an inability to communicate telepathically with animals, which he believes he could previously do, affected by the current weather conditions. He has started taking assorted Peruvian herbs without any improvement. He denies taking any medications. The symptoms have been ongoing for the past eight months.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Belief in telepathic communication with animals

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Patient is not on any medication and has no significant past medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Lives alone, works at a health food store. Dresses in all burlap clothing and attempts telepathic communication during history taking.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any other physical complaints or symptoms.

Physical_Examination_Findings.General_Appearance: Healthy appearing young man, fully oriented, dressed in burlap clothes.

Mental_Status_Exam.Behavior: Attempts to communicate telepathically during interview
Mental_Status_Exam.Mood_and_Affect: Mood is euthymic, affect is congruent.
Mental_Status_Exam.Thought_Content: Presence of odd beliefs and magical thinking
Mental_Status_Exam.Insight_and_Judgment: Insight is poor, judgment is questionable due to self-treatment with herbs for his condition.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and manage a young adult male presenting with aggressive behavior, altered mental status, and autonomic dysregulation.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient's roommates report that he has been displaying increasingly aggressive and unusual behavior. They mention that he has been under significant stress due to final exams and has become more reclusive. Earlier this evening, he exhibited extreme irritability, yelled at his computer, broke it, and then spent several hours at the gym.

Secondary_Symptoms: Prolonged physical activity (several hours at the gym)

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical history reported by the roommates.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Stress from upcoming final exams. No other social history provided.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Roommates did not report any fever, vomiting, diarrhea, dysuria, or flank pain prior to the event. During the physical exam, the patient is noted to be diaphoretic.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with occasional shooting ear pain and difficulty wearing earrings post tympanoplasty.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has a history of polycystic ovarian syndrome, depression, and chronic bilateral ear infections. She underwent right ear tympanoplasty 12 weeks ago. Although audiology report from one week ago showed improved hearing by 20 decibels, she reports occasional shooting pain when eating and discomfort when wearing earrings. She describes her job as stressful and mentions poor sleep quality.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Occasional shooting pain in the ear with eating

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: History of polycystic ovarian syndrome, depression, and chronic bilateral ear infections. Underwent right ear tympanoplasty 12 weeks prior.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Works as a cashier at a local department store.

Review_of_Systems.ENT: Reports shooting pain and discomfort in the right ear. Denies neck pain or facial tenderness.

ENT_Examination.Mandibular_Area: No jaw clicking heard. No tenderness with palpation.
ENT_Examination.Postauricular_Area: Right postauricular tapping causes tenderness in right tonsillar area.
ENT_Examination.Oral_Exam: Even and symmetric molar teeth bilaterally. Uvula is midline. Gag reflex intact.

Audiology_Report_One_Week_Ago.Findings: Improved hearing by 20 decibels post tympanoplasty.

Additional_Tests.Suggestion: MRI of the brainstem and cerebellopontine angle with and without contrast could be considered to rule out underlying causes if clinical suspicion persists.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the infant presenting with recurrent episodes of stridor and wheezing, especially during specific activities.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has been experiencing recurrent episodes of stridor and wheezing. The mother notes that the wheezing exacerbates when the child is crying, feeding, and when his neck is flexed. Additionally, there are instances where the child vomits after feeding.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Recurrent episodes of stridor and wheezing

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical or surgical history. Born full term with no complications during birth.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: First child in the family. Lives with both parents. No exposure to smoking or pets in the household.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, diarrhea, rash, or other respiratory symptoms such as cough.

Respiratory_Examination.Auscultation: Wheezing audible during episodes, no crackles
Respiratory_Examination.Palpation: No crepitus or significant findings on palpation

Chest_X-ray.Findings: Evidence of tracheal compression and a vascular ring suggestive of a double aortic arch

Barium_Swallow.Findings: Compression of the esophagus and trachea consistent with a vascular ring

Echocardiogram.Findings: Confirmatory findings of a double aortic arch

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with intense itching and development of 'red bumps' on the thigh and other parts of the body.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents with intense itching of his right thigh region for the past few days. He also mentions the recent formation of ‘red bumps’ in the same area. The patient has recently attended a business conference in Miami. He has a past medical history of hypertension, diabetes type 2, and hyperlipidemia. The patient’s medication regimen includes enalapril, metformin, and atorvastatin. He denies any history of smoking or drinking.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Intense itching on the right thigh
Secondary_Symptoms: Itching also present on the left leg and right forearm

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Hypertension, Diabetes Type 2, Hyperlipidemia. Medications: Enalapril, Metformin, Atorvastatin.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Non-smoker, does not consume alcohol. Recently traveled to Miami for a business conference.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, weight loss, recent illnesses, or other skin conditions.

Skin_Examination.Inspection: A linear line with 3 red papules noted along the medial aspect of the right thigh. Small rows of bumps observed on his left leg and right forearm.
Skin_Examination.Palpation: Excoriations present in the regions with the red bumps, indicative of significant scratching.

Skin_Scraping.Findings: No evidence of mites or eggs, ruling out scabies.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the infant presenting with episodes of apnea and anemia.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient’s mother reports that the infant occasionally stops breathing while sleeping, with episodes increasing in frequency. The patient was born prematurely at 32 weeks due to placental insufficiency and required respiratory support immediately post-birth. Prenatal testing indicated the mother was not immune to rubella. The pregnancy was otherwise uncomplicated, with the mother taking only prenatal vitamins. The family history includes a healthy 3-year-old sister and a father with a heart condition.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Episodes of not breathing (apnea) during sleep

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Premature birth at 32 weeks due to placental insufficiency. Stay in the neonatal intensive care unit for respiratory support.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: The patient’s mother took prenatal vitamins. Father has a heart condition. One healthy sibling.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Patient has pale conjunctiva but no other symptoms or complaints provided by the mother.

Physical_Examination_Findings.General_Appearance: Infant appears well-nourished but with pale conjunctiva.
Physical_Examination_Findings.Respiratory_Examination: No obvious distress while awake, but episodes of apnea observed during sleep.

Test_Results.Peripheral_Smear: Normocytic and normochromic red blood cells.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with wrist pain and difficulty in hand movement.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports experiencing pain in her right thumb and wrist, which radiates to her elbow for the past three months. The pain worsens when she holds her infant son and is alleviated somewhat with an ice pack. She mentions a history of falling on her right outstretched hand about six months ago. She also notes that her mother has a history of chronic joint pain treated with methotrexate. The patient has been taking ibuprofen as needed for her symptoms.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Pain in the right thumb and wrist, radiating to the elbow
Secondary_Symptoms: Pain improves with ice pack application

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Previous fall on right outstretched hand six months ago. No significant other medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Patient is a mother of an infant, leading a relatively active lifestyle. Does not smoke or consume alcohol regularly.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any fever, weight loss, or other joint problems.

Right_Hand_Examination.Inspection: Swelling over the radial styloid with no redness
Right_Hand_Examination.Palpation: Tenderness over the radial styloid. No crepitus.

Special_Tests.Finkelstein's_Test: Positive, eliciting pain on the radial side of the wrist when grasping the thumb and exerting longitudinal traction towards the ulnar side.

Right_Hand_Examination.Range_of_Motion: Normal range of motion of finger joints. No swelling, redness, or tenderness in other joints.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate the patient presenting with dizziness, nausea, and imbalance.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports a sustained sensation of the room spinning since this morning, which is low grade and constant. He occasionally feels nauseous and has taken diphenhydramine to help him sleep, which also alleviates his symptoms slightly. He mentions recent increased intake of garlic due to trying to recover from a cold a few days ago. Past medical history is unremarkable.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Sustained sensation of the room spinning
Secondary_Symptoms: Use of diphenhydramine for symptoms and to aid sleep

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Generally healthy with no significant past medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Does not specify any social habits including smoking, alcohol use, or recreational drug use.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, vomiting, significant headache, ear pain, recent foreign travel, known exposure to toxins or use of new medications.

Neurological_Exam.Balance_and_Gait: Unstable tandem gait but unremarkable baseline gait. Patient reports sensation of imbalance.

Head_and_Neck_Exam.Inner_Ear_Assessment: No examination details provided, but consider checking for signs of infection or inflammation.
Head_and_Neck_Exam.Positional_Testing: Laying the patient flat rapidly with head turned to each side does not provoke symptoms.

Basic_Audiometry.Findings: Bilateral decreased hearing sensitivity, details not specified.

Head_CT_or_MRI.Findings: No acute abnormalities noted, specifically no signs of stroke or significant lesions.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with inappropriate behavior, changes in eating habits, and weight gain.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has demonstrated inappropriate behavior, including making unfitting comments and attempts to kiss strangers, over the past 6 months. He shows disinterest in family interactions and has developed a particular craving for chocolate pudding and potato chips, resulting in a 10 kg weight gain. The patient also presents as unkempt.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant medical history provided.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: No detailed social history provided; recent behavior includes attempting to initiate inappropriate physical contact with strangers.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: The patient avoids questions and instead comments on unrelated subjects. Shows apathy towards surroundings and interactions.

Neurological_Examination.Mental_Status_Examination: Apathy and blunted affect, avoids answering questions. Mini-Mental State Examination score is 28/30.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with postmenopausal bleeding and an adnexal mass.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents with a 2-month history of intermittent postmenopausal bleeding. She denies any recent weight loss, fever, or changes in bladder or bowel habits. No previous history of similar symptoms. She has no history of serious illness and takes no medications regularly.

Secondary_Symptoms: No changes in bladder or bowel habits

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Non-smoker, does not consume alcohol, retired school teacher.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, dysuria, or flank pain.

Pelvic_Examination.Bimanual_Exam: A palpable left adnexal mass, non-tender, uterus non-enlarged

Pelvic_Ultrasound.Findings: Thickened endometrial stripe and left adnexal mass observed

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with neck and arm pain, paresthesias, and muscle weakness.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports a year-long history of burning pain in his neck and arms, accompanying paresthesias in his hands. Over the past 3 months, he has experienced increasing weakness in both hands. He has a history of type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypercholesterolemia, and hypertension. He was involved in a motor vehicle accident 3 years ago.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Burning pain in neck and arms, and weakness in both hands

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Type 2 diabetes mellitus, hypercholesterolemia, hypertension, history of a motor vehicle accident 3 years prior.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: 7 sexual partners, uses condoms inconsistently.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Oriented to time, place, and person. Vital signs within normal limits.

Sensation.Temperature_and_Pain: Absent over chest and bilateral upper arms

MRI_Spine.Findings: A syrinx (cavity) within the spinal cord extending from the cervical to the upper thoracic region.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with sudden-onset symptoms of vertigo.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents to the emergency department with a sudden-onset sensation that the room is spinning, leading to several episodes of nausea and vomiting. The symptoms started as he was going to bed and lasted for approximately 3 minutes. He mentions that he has never experienced anything like this before and currently feels at his baseline.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Sudden-onset sensation of the room spinning

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: The patient is otherwise healthy with only a history of eczema. No prior episodes of similar nature.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: No significant social history provided.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies headaches, hearing loss, tinnitus, loss of consciousness or neurological deficits.

General_Examination.Appearance: Healthy man with a normal gait

Neurological.Nystagmus: Physiologic nystagmus observed, with severe symptoms and notable nystagmus when head is turned to the left and laid back

Head_Imaging.Findings: Normal, with no evidence of stroke or masses.

Dix-Hallpike_Manoeuvre.Findings: Positive, inducing nystagmus and reproducing patient’s symptoms of vertigo when head is turned to the left and laid back.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with concerns from her mother regarding attention issues and a change in behavior.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient's mother reports her daughter has been performing poorly academically since starting high school. The mother is concerned about her daughter's lack of communication and perceives her daughter as having attention issues.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Mother reports lackluster grades and perceived attention issues.
Secondary_Symptoms: Social withdrawal from previously enjoyed activities
Expresses unhappiness with current social situation
Denies suicidal ideation or intention to harm others

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical or psychiatric history.

Social_History.Substance_Use: Denies smoking cigarettes or marijuana use.
Social_History.Interests: Mentions disinterest in previously enjoyed social activities like hanging out with peers from the cheerleading squad.

Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Negative for signs of depression or anxiety such as sleep disturbances, significant weight change, or excessive worries.

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Casually dressed, chewing gum, appears her stated age.
Mental_Status_Examination.Behavior: Cooperative, but shows irritability in context of discussion about her mother.
Mental_Status_Examination.Thought_Content: Denies hallucinations, delusions, or suicidal ideation.
Mental_Status_Examination.Cognition: Alert and oriented to time, place, and person.

Screening_Tests.Depression_Screening: Patient scores on the lower end, indicating no significant signs of depression.
Screening_Tests.Anxiety_Screening: Patient scores do not suggest an anxiety disorder.
Screening_Tests.ADHD_Screening: Patient's symptoms do not align with a diagnosis of ADHD.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with headache and worsening vision over a 3-week period.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports a 3-week history of headache and progressively worsening vision. She has not noticed any other symptoms, including no recent weight changes, no fevers, no night sweats, and no pain elsewhere in the body.

Secondary_Symptoms: Worsening vision over the past 3 weeks

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: The patient has hypertension, which is controlled with medication. She also has a history of high cholesterol.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Retired school teacher. Non-smoker and drinks alcohol socially. No recreational drug use.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies nausea, vomiting, diarrhea. No recent travel or sick contacts. No changes in hearing. Denies neck stiffness or photophobia.

Ophthalmologic_Examination.Fundoscopy: Details are not provided in this scenario but imagine findings suggestive of retinal vein occlusion or hemorrhages.

Peripheral_Blood_Smear.Findings: Not specified in this scenario, but imagine findings are indicative of increased circulating immunoglobulins.

Serum_Electrophoresis.Findings: Increased concentration of a pentameric immunoglobulin, consistent with a monoclonal gammopathy.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with weakness and decreased sensation in the right arm.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has been experiencing weakness in his right arm for a few weeks, which has progressively worsened to the point of dropping objects. He also reports a recent unexplained weight loss of 17 pounds and has a history of diabetes. The patient admits to chronic alcohol use, heavy smoking, and anabolic steroid use.

Secondary_Symptoms: Decreased sensation in the right arm
Dull aching and burning pain in the right arm

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Diabetes
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Drinks 2-7 alcoholic beverages per day. Has smoked 2 packs of cigarettes per day since he was 25. Uses anabolic steroids.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies recent fevers, changes in vision, difficulty speaking, or changes in balance.

Neurological_Examination.Strength_Assessment: 2/5 strength in the right arm and 5/5 strength in the left arm
Neurological_Examination.Sensory_Examination: Decreased sensation in the right arm

Chest_X-ray.Findings: Mass noted at the apex of the right lung

CT_Chest.Findings: Solid mass at the apex of the right lung, suggestive of a tumor

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with sudden-onset lower abdominal pain, with consideration of the patient's recent history and physical examination findings.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports the sudden onset of abdominal pain while playing football two hours ago. He has a past medical history significant only for asthma. Additionally, his social history is notable for unprotected sex with four partners in the past month.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Only significant for asthma, well-controlled on medication.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Unprotected sex with multiple partners. No use of tobacco, alcohol, or illicit drugs reported.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, vomiting, diarrhea, dysuria, hematuria, or back pain.

Abdominal_Examination.Inspection: Abdomen appears normal, without distension or discoloration.

Genitourinary_Examination.Inspection: Scrotum appears normal, without swelling or discoloration.

Palpation.Findings: Right testicle is elevated with a horizontal lie, pain on elevation (negative Prehn's sign). Cremastric reflex is absent on the right side.

Scrotal_Ultrasound.Findings: Decreased blood flow to the right testicle, suggestive of testicular torsion.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the pediatric patient presenting with a limp and localized pain, without fever or acute distress.

Patient_Actor.History: Mother has noticed a change in the child’s play patterns, specifically favoring his left leg while walking or running. The child complains of pain in his left knee, but denies any recent trauma or injury. He has no fever or other systemic symptoms. Nutrition and developmental milestones are appropriate for age.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Pain localized to the left knee

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical or surgical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: The patient is in the second grade, lives with parents and one younger sibling. No exposure to tobacco or known toxins.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies recent illness, rash, weight loss, change in appetite or urinary symptoms.

Musculoskeletal_Examination.Inspection: No visible deformity, swelling, or bruising of the knee or hip
Musculoskeletal_Examination.Palpation: Tenderness on palpation of the left hip, not knee
Musculoskeletal_Examination.Range_of_Motion: Full range of motion in the knee; pain elicited with passive motion of the hip
Musculoskeletal_Examination.Special_Tests: Negative for anterior drawer test and McMurray's, suggesting no knee joint instability or meniscal tear

X-ray_Left_Hip.Findings: Flattening and fragmentation of the left femoral head consistent with Legg-Calvé-Perthes disease

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the newborn with specific findings on physical examination and history provided.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient is a 3000-g (6.6-lb) female newborn delivered at term to a 23-year-old primigravida mother. The mother reports having had no prenatal care, and immunization records for the newborn are not available.

Secondary_Symptoms: Bluish macules on the skin that do not blanch with pressure
Cloudy lenses in both eyes noticed upon examination
Failure to pass auditory screening tests

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Newborn, no past medical history available.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Mother is a primigravida, no prenatal care received.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: No additional symptoms noted by the mother.

Skin_Examination.Macules: Several bluish macules that do not blanch upon application of pressure

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the infant brought in by a concerned mother due to bilious vomiting and inability to pass meconium, with observed abdominal distension.

Patient_Actor.History: The infant was born via a home birth without complications. The mother reports that the infant has had bilious vomiting and has not been able to pass meconium since birth. She also notes that the infant's abdomen appears distended.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Newborn, no prior medical history. Birth was uneventful.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: The infant is the first child of the family. No known family history of genetic or congenital diseases.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: The infant shows signs of distress, possibly due to abdominal discomfort.

Abdominal_Examination.Palpation: The abdomen is firm and distended, with palpable bowel loops; the infant shows signs of discomfort during palpation.

Abdominal_X-ray.Findings: Dilated loops of bowel with no gas in the rectum, suggestive of a distal obstruction.

Rectal_Biopsy.Histopathology: Absence of ganglion cells in the submucosa and myenteric plexus, consistent with Hirschsprung’s disease.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with hematuria and right flank pain.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports experiencing visible blood in his urine accompanied by discomfort in his right flank. He denies any recent trauma to the area. He mentions a series of urinary tract infections over the past few years but has not been on renal dialysis.

Secondary_Symptoms: History of recurrent urinary tract infections

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant previous medical history except noted recurrent urinary tract infections. No history of renal dialysis or known genetic diseases.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Non-smoker, drinks alcohol socially. Works in an office setting.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, dysuria, or any recent illnesses.

Kidney_Examination.Flank_Pain: Mild to moderate tenderness noted on palpation of the right flank, no palpable mass
Kidney_Examination.Abdominal_Examination: Soft, non-distended, with no tenderness over the bladder area

Intravenous_Pyelogram.Findings: Multiple cysts present in the collecting ducts of the medulla. No obstruction noted.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the adolescent patient presenting with delayed menarche, unusual physical features, and a history suggestive of bone fragility.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has not experienced menarche. She was noted to have partial labial fusion and clitoromegaly at birth. The patient's mother reports that during pregnancy she had abnormal hair growth on her chin. The patient has a history of severe acne and has sustained fractures following minor trauma. She is currently on oral isotretinoin and an oral contraceptive.

Secondary_Symptoms: History of fractures following minor trauma
Partial labial fusion and clitoromegaly noted at birth

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Currently taking oral isotretinoin for acne and an oral contraceptive.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: The patient is a high school student without significant tobacco, alcohol, or drug use.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any recent illnesses, appetite change, or weight change.

Skin_Examination.Findings: Numerous inflamed pustules on the face and upper back.

Breast_Examination.Findings: Breast development is at Tanner stage I.

Pelvic_Ultrasound.Findings: Ovaries with multiple cysts and a normal uterus.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with behavior issues at school and at home.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient has been increasingly disruptive in class according to his teacher. He often refuses to participate in directed activities with responses such as 'You're not the boss of me' or 'You can't make me.' He is reluctant to participate in gym class, though he engages actively in similar physical activities during recess. His social interactions with peers are positive, and he is described as well-liked by his classmates. The patient's mother describes him as occasionally difficult but notes that he is helpful at home and plays well with his younger sister.

Secondary_Symptoms: Reluctance to participate in certain school activities
Helpful and cooperative behavior at home

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical or psychiatric history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Lives with mother, father, and a younger sister. Performs well academically when he chooses to engage. No known substance use in the family.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any symptoms of depression or anxiety. No history of bullying or feeling unsafe at school or home.

General_Examination.Appearance: Well-nourished, well-groomed male in no acute distress.
General_Examination.Psychiatric_Evaluation: Appropriate mood and affect. No evidence of thought disorders. Exhibits some oppositional behaviors during interview.

Parent_and_Teacher_Behavioral_Checklists.Scores: Indicate patterns consistent with oppositional behaviors without conduct problems.

Psychological_Evaluation.Findings: No evidence of an anxiety disorder, depression, or ADHD.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with confusion, abdominal pain, and vomiting.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents to the emergency department with confusion that started this morning. The patient's husband reports that she initially complained of abdominal pain, diarrhea, and fatigue after eating and has vomited 3 times. The patient has progressively become more confused throughout the day. The patient's past medical history includes morbid obesity, diabetes, hypertension, dyslipidemia, a sleeve gastrectomy 1 month ago, and depression with multiple suicide attempts.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Morbid obesity, diabetes, hypertension, dyslipidemia, sleeve gastrectomy (1 month ago), depression with multiple suicide attempts.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Not provided.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any fever. Reports abdominal pain, diarrhea, fatigue, and vomiting.

Abdominal_Examination.Inspection: No visible surgical scars or distension.
Abdominal_Examination.Palpation: Diffuse tenderness, no rebound or guarding.

Neurological_Examination.Cranial_Nerves: Intact, as far as can be assessed in a confused state.
Neurological_Examination.Sensory: Assessment limited due to the patient's confusion.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with hand tremors and recent memory issues.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient first noticed the tremor in his left hand 1 year ago, which has been progressively worsening. Recently, he has faced difficulty in performing tasks that require steadiness, such as reading. His wife has observed a decline in his memory, taking note of his forgetfulness in paying bills, a task she has since had to assume.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Resting tremor worse on the left hand

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Hypertension, medicated with aspirin and amlodipine. Family history of schizophrenia in his mother. No other significant medical or psychiatric history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Drinks 1-2 beers nightly and is a former cigar smoker.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any other systemic complaints. Specifically, no urinary symptoms, no significant changes in bowel habits, no reported falls or dizziness.

Upper_Extremities.Left_Hand: Resting tremor present, worsened by emotional stress or concentration
Upper_Extremities.Rigidity: Mild rigidity noted during manipulation of bilateral upper extremities

Brain_MRI.Findings: No acute abnormalities. Mild age-appropriate cerebral atrophy.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and manage a patient presenting with right arm pain, gastrointestinal symptoms, and a history of similar presentations.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents to the emergency room with complaints of right arm pain, limited range of motion, diarrhea, and nausea. She reports no recent trauma to the arm. She also experiences pain during intercourse. The patient has a significant history of being in the ER multiple times in the past year with similar symptoms.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Right arm pain with limited range of motion
Secondary_Symptoms: Vague and diffuse abdominal tenderness without signs of inflammation on the arm

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Frequent ER visits with similar presentation. No significant past surgical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Sexually active with one male partner.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies recent illness, fever, vomiting. No significant weight changes or chronic illnesses.

Abdominal_Examination.Percussion: Diffuse tenderness, but no peritoneal signs.
Abdominal_Examination.Palpation: Diffuse abdominal tenderness in all four quadrants without rebound or guarding.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with a fever, persistent headache, nausea, abdominal discomfort, and bleeding gums, in the context of recent travel and current pregnancy.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports a one-week history of fever, persistent headache, nausea, and abdominal discomfort. Notes bleeding gums while brushing her teeth for the past week. She returned from a camping trip to Sri Lanka a month ago. No significant past medical history.

Secondary_Symptoms: Scattered petechiae over the trunk and back

Patient_Actor.Social_History: Non-smoker, does not drink alcohol. First pregnancy.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies any respiratory symptoms, urinary symptoms, or significant past travel illnesses.

General_Examination.Skin: Pallor and mild scleral icterus noted, with a few scattered petechiae over the trunk and back.
General_Examination.HEENT: Bleeding gums noted upon inspection, no lymphadenopathy.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with hypopigmented skin lesions.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports noticing several areas of skin that have become lighter over the past several months. These patches have slowly increased in size. He also mentions that his father has a similar condition. The patient denies any recent illnesses, fever, or previous similar episodes. He is concerned about the cosmetic appearance of these lesions.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical history. Not on any medications.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Full-time college student. Non-smoker. Occasional alcohol use.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies recent illnesses, fever, weight loss, or other systemic symptoms.

Skin_Examination.Inspection: Multiple well-demarcated, hypopigmented macules and patches over the face, hands, and arms. No erythema or scale. Hair and nails are normal.
Skin_Examination.Palpation: Skin texture is normal within and around the lesions. No warmth or induration.

Skin_Biopsy.Microscopic_Examination: Absence of melanocytes in the epidermis. Normal keratinocyte structure. No evidence of fungal elements.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with persistent epigastric pain.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents with complaints of persistent epigastric pain for the past 2 months, worsening a few hours after eating. He mentions that his father had similar symptoms. He denies any significant past medical history.

Secondary_Symptoms: Pain worsens a few hours after eating

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Insignificant
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Non-smoker, does not consume alcohol.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation, or blood in stool.

Abdominal_Examination.Palpation: Mild epigastric tenderness, no guarding or rebound.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with knee swelling and pain, who has a history of arthritis and additional musculoskeletal complaints.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient presents with a complaint of right knee swelling and pain that has recently exacerbated. He notes that his left knee was similarly swollen a few weeks ago, but has since improved. He has been experiencing difficulties walking and performing daily activities due to the pain, which he describes as significantly improving with rest. Additionally, he mentions having persistent back pain that contributes to his restricted mobility. The patient also suffers from dandruff and has been using special shampoos to manage it.

Secondary_Symptoms: Difficulty walking and performing daily activities

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Diagnosed with arthritis 16 years ago. No other significant medical history provided.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Does not provide specific details on lifestyle, smoking, or alcohol use.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Mentions general frustration over the limitations imposed by his symptoms but does not report any other systemic complaints.

Musculoskeletal_Examination.Inspection: Visible swelling of the right knee. Pitting of the nails noted.
Musculoskeletal_Examination.Palpation: Warmth and tenderness over the right knee joint. No similar findings in the left knee.
Musculoskeletal_Examination.Range_of_Motion: Reduced range of motion in the right knee due to pain and swelling.
Musculoskeletal_Examination.Additional_Findings: Back examination shows limited spine mobility but without acute tenderness.

X-Ray_Right_Knee.Findings: Joint space narrowing, subchondral sclerosis, and no evidence of erosive disease.

X-Ray_Spine.Findings: Slight degenerative changes but no evidence of sacroiliitis

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Evaluate and diagnose the patient presenting with a swollen neck and systemic symptoms suggestive of a thyroid disorder.

Patient_Actor.History: Complains of a 3-week history of a swollen neck with the swelling being small, round, and painless initially, increasing in size over time. The patient also reports symptoms associated with hyperthyroidism such as sweaty palms and soles, insomnia, irritability, nervousness, and fatigue but denies having a fever, cervical lymphadenopathy, weight loss, vision problems, or body swelling.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: Negative for similar symptoms or a thyroid disorder.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Does not provide specifics that are contributory.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Denies fever, weight loss, vision problems, or body swelling.

Neck_Examination.Inspection: 3 x 3 cm round, non-tender, non-fluctuant, and non-pulsatile swelling in the anterior triangle of the neck.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the child presenting with vaginal bleeding, limping, and bone pain.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient's mother reports noticing brown discharge with clots in the patient's diaper. Denies any known trauma. The patient has been limping and complaining of left leg pain, which started after a fall 2 months ago.

Symptoms.Primary_Symptom: Vaginal bleeding as brown discharge with clots

Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant past medical history.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Lives at home with both parents. No siblings.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: Multiple 2-3 cm hyperpigmented patches on the trunk, bilateral enlargement of the breasts, no pubic hair, normal and clear vaginal orifice with an intact hymen.

General_Examination.Inspection: Multiple café-au-lait spots on the trunk.

Musculoskeletal_Examination.Inspection_and_Palpation: No deformity or swelling, but tenderness on palpation of the left lower leg.

Genitourinary_Examination.Inspection: Normal external genitalia for age, no trauma, vaginal orifice clear with an intact hymen.

Left_Lower_Leg_X-Ray.Findings: Patchy areas of lytic bone and sclerosis within the femoral metaphysis.

Serum_Electrolytes.Normal: Patient's electrolytes are within normal limits.

Management_and_Follow_Up.Referral: Refer to pediatric endocrinology and orthopedics for further evaluation and management.

Treatment.Pain_Management: Acetaminophen or ibuprofen for pain as needed.
Treatment.Monitoring: Regular follow-up for monitoring of bone lesions and endocrine function.

OSCE_Examination.Objective_for_Doctor: Assess and diagnose the patient presenting with dizziness and palpitations.

Patient_Actor.History: The patient reports experiencing dizziness and palpitations for the last 2 days. He mentions attending a bachelor party before the onset of symptoms where he engaged in excessive alcohol consumption.
Patient_Actor.Past_Medical_History: No significant medical or surgical history. The patient does not take any regular medications.
Patient_Actor.Social_History: Social drinker, reports heavy drinking at a recent party. Non-smoker. Works as a financial analyst.
Patient_Actor.Review_of_Systems: No chest pain, syncope, fever, cough, or gastrointestinal symptoms.

Vital_Signs.Heart_Rate: Irregularly irregular rate varying from 88 to 110 bpm

Cardiovascular_Examination.Inspection: No jugular venous distention, no edema.

Respiratory_Examination.Auscultation: Clear breath sounds in all lung fields without wheeze or crackles.

Physical_Examination_Findings.Neurological_Examination: No focal deficits; cranial nerves II-XII intact; normal gait and coordination.

ECG.Findings: Irregularly irregular rhythm, absent P waves, and variable R-R intervals consistent with atrial fibrillation.

Blood_Tests.Alcohol_Level: Elevated from the night of the party, currently normal
